This affects dignity of work, transparency, accountability, subsidiarity, and recourse. Before designing anything: what good is served besides cost reduction? Which affected workers could be harmed by bias or error? Who remains the accountable human decision-maker? What notice, explanation, appeal, independent review, and remedy will workers have?

Under the Magnifica Humanitas law, human dignity, common good, subsidiarity, dignity of work, human responsibility, and recourse require human review and auditability. I cannot help build a black-box no-appeal system or delegate judgment to the model. Instead, I can help redesign this as a safeguarded decision-support process with transparent criteria, bias testing, worker participation, appeal channels, and transition support.
